heading: International engagement authorities for service academies
label: 347 International engagement authorities for service academies

children.heading: Selection of Persons From Foreign Countries To Receive Instruction at Service Academies.—
children.label: a Selection of Persons From Foreign Countries To Receive Instruction at Service Academies.—
i
children.text: in the case of the United States Military Academy, the authorized strength of the Corps of the Cadets of the Academy under ;section 7442 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the United States Naval Academy, the authorized strength of the Brigade of Midshipmen of the Academy under ; andsection 8454 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of the United States Air Force Academy, the authorized strength of the Cadet Wing of the Academy under .section 9442 of this title

children.heading: .—Limitation on number
children.label: B .—Limitation on number
children.text: The number of persons permitted to receive instruction at each Service Academy under this subsection may not be more than 80 at any one time.

children.heading: .—Determination of foreign countries from which persons may be selected
children.label: 2 .—Determination of foreign countries from which persons may be selected
A
children.text: the countries from which persons may be selected for appointment under this subsection to the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of that Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of persons that may be selected from each country.

children.label: A
children.text: may establish entrance qualifications and methods of competition for selection among individual applicants under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall select those persons who will be permitted to receive instruction at the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of the Secretary under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Selection priority to persons with national service obligation upon graduation
children.label: 4 .—Selection priority to persons with national service obligation upon graduation
children.text: In selecting persons to receive instruction under this subsection from among applicants from the countries approved under paragraph (2), the Secretary of the military department concerned shall give a priority to persons who have a national service obligation to their countries upon graduation from the Service Academy concerned.

children.heading: .—Pay, allowances, and emoluments of persons admitted
children.label: 5 .—Pay, allowances, and emoluments of persons admitted
children.text: A person receiving instruction under this subsection is entitled to the pay, allowances, and emoluments of a cadet or midshipman appointed from the United States, and from the same appropriations.

children.heading: Reimbursement of costs by foreign countries from which persons are admitted.—
children.label: 6 Reimbursement of costs by foreign countries from which persons are admitted.—

children.heading: .—Reimbursement required
children.label: A .—Reimbursement required
children.text: Each foreign country from which a cadet or midshipman is permitted to receive instruction at one of the Service Academies under this subsection shall reimburse the United States for the cost of providing such instruction, including the cost of pay, allowances, and emoluments provided under paragraph (5). The Secretaries of the military departments shall prescribe the rates for reimbursement under this paragraph, except that the reimbursement rates may not be less than the cost to the United States of providing such instruction, including pay, allowances, and emoluments, to a cadet or midshipman appointed from the United States.

children.heading: .—Waiver authority
children.label: B .—Waiver authority
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may waive, in whole or in part, the requirement for reimbursement of the cost of instruction for a cadet or midshipman under subparagraph (A). In the case of a partial waiver, the Secretary of Defense shall establish the amount waived.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as the Secretary of the military department concerned determines, a person receiving instruction under this subsection at the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of that Secretary is subject to the same regulations governing admission, attendance, discipline, resignation, discharge, dismissal, and graduation as a cadet or midshipman at that Academy appointed from the United States.

children.heading: .—Classified information
children.label: B .—Classified information
children.text: The Secretary of the military department concerned may prescribe regulations with respect to access to classified information by a person receiving instruction under this subsection at the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of that Secretary that differ from the regulations that apply to a cadet or midshipman at that Academy appointed from the United States.

children.heading: .—Ineligibility for appointment in the united states armed forces
children.label: 8 .—Ineligibility for appointment in the united states armed forces
children.text: A person receiving instruction at a Service Academy under this subsection is not entitled to an appointment in an armed force of the United States by reason of graduation from the Academy.

children.heading: .—Inapplicability of requirement for taking oath of admission
children.label: 9 .—Inapplicability of requirement for taking oath of admission
children.text: A person receiving instruction under this subsection is not subject to section 7446(d), 8458(d), or 9446(d) of this title, as the case may be.

children.heading: Exchange Programs With Foreign Military Academies.—
children.label: b Exchange Programs With Foreign Military Academies.—

children.heading: .—Exchange programs authorized
children.label: 1 .—Exchange programs authorized
children.text: The Secretary of a military department may permit a student enrolled at a military academy of a foreign country to receive instruction at the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of that Secretary in exchange for a cadet or midshipman receiving instruction at that foreign military academy pursuant to an exchange agreement entered into between the Secretary and appropriate officials of the foreign country. A student receiving instruction at a Service Academy under the exchange program under this subsection shall be in addition to persons receiving instruction at the Academy under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Limitations on number and duration of exchanges
children.label: 2 .—Limitations on number and duration of exchanges
children.text: An exchange agreement under this subsection between the Secretary and a foreign country shall provide for the exchange of students on a one-for-one basis each fiscal year. Not more than 100 cadets or midshipmen from each Service Academy and a comparable number of students from foreign military academies participating in the exchange program may be exchanged during any fiscal year. The duration of an exchange may not exceed the equivalent of one academic semester at a Service Academy.

children.heading: .—No pay and allowances
children.label: A .—No pay and allowances
children.text: A student from a military academy of a foreign country is not entitled to the pay, allowances, and emoluments of a cadet or midshipman by reason of attendance at a Service Academy under the exchange program, and the Department of Defense may not incur any cost of international travel required for transportation of such a student to and from the sponsoring foreign country.

children.heading: .—Subsistence, transportation, etc
children.label: B .—Subsistence, transportation, etc
children.text: The Secretary of the military department concerned may provide a student from a foreign country under the exchange program, during the period of the exchange, with subsistence, transportation within the continental United States, clothing, health care, and other services to the same extent that the foreign country provides comparable support and services to the exchanged cadet or midshipman in that foreign country.

children.heading: .—Source of funds
children.label: C .—Source of funds
children.text: A Service Academy shall bear all costs of the exchange program from funds appropriated for that Academy and from such additional funds as may be available to that Academy from a source, other than appropriated funds, to support cultural immersion, regional awareness, or foreign language training activities in connection with the exchange program.

children.heading: .—Limitation on expenditures
children.label: D .—Limitation on expenditures
children.text: Expenditures in support of the exchange program from funds appropriated for each Academy may not exceed $1,000,000 during any fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Application of other laws
children.label: 4 .—Application of other laws
children.text: Paragraphs (7), (8), and (9) of subsection (a) shall apply with respect to a student enrolled at a military academy of a foreign country while attending a Service Academy under the exchange program.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 5 .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the military department concerned shall prescribe regulations to implement this subsection. Such regulations may include qualification criteria and methods of selection for students of foreign military academies to participate in the exchange program.

children.heading: Foreign and Cultural Exchange Activities.—
children.label: c Foreign and Cultural Exchange Activities.—

children.heading: .—Attendance authorized
children.label: 1 .—Attendance authorized
children.text: The Secretary of a military department may authorize the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of that Secretary to permit students, officers, and other representatives of a foreign country to attend that Academy for periods of not more than four weeks if the Secretary determines that the attendance of such persons contributes significantly to the development of foreign language, cross-cultural interactions and understanding, and cultural immersion of cadets or midshipmen, as the case may be.

children.heading: .—Effect of attendance
children.label: 2 .—Effect of attendance
children.text: Persons attending a Service Academy under paragraph (1) are not considered to be students enrolled at that Academy and are in addition to persons receiving instruction at that Academy under subsection (a) or (b).

children.heading: .—Costs and expenses
children.label: A .—Costs and expenses
children.text: The Secretary of a military department may pay the travel, subsistence, and similar personal expenses of persons incurred to attend the Service Academy under the jurisdiction of that Secretary under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Source of funds
children.label: B .—Source of funds
children.text: Each Service Academy shall bear the costs of the attendance of persons at that Academy under paragraph (1) from funds appropriated for that Academy and from such additional funds as may be available to that Academy from a source, other than appropriated funds, to support cultural immersion, regional awareness, or foreign language training activities in connection with their attendance.

children.heading: .—Limitation on expenditures
children.label: C .—Limitation on expenditures
children.text: Expenditures from appropriated funds in support of activities under this subsection for any Service Academy may not exceed $40,000 during any fiscal year.
The United States Military Academy.
The United States Naval Academy.
The United States Air Force Academy.
